components.interpretation: The papers name an input gray level I(x,y), but do not freeze channel conversion, sample depth, polarity, dtype, clipping, or decode behavior.
open_questions: What exact byte-level image decoding and grayscale convention was used?

components.interpretation: Fig. 6 says the background was segmented and the experiment rejects incomplete ROIs, but the segmentation, mask use, and pixel eligibility algorithms are not given.
open_questions: Are non-foreground pixels excluded from normalization, convolution, and AAD?

components.interpretation: The reference point is the point of maximum curvature of concave ridges.

A multi-resolution orientation-field detector is outlined, but it permits different gradient operators, gives RI/RII geometry only pictorially, and omits block anchoring, segmentation, borders, ties, and search-neighborhood geometry.
open_questions: What are the exact RI and RII dimensions/angles?
Which gradient operator and border rule are authoritative?
How are ties and invalid foreground blocks handled?

components.interpretation: CVPR defines a local-symmetry axis, but says it is not used; TIP assumes vertically oriented acquisition and defers automatic orientation determination.
open_questions: Is a reference orientation part of the actual matcher contract?
If so, what sign, zero axis, and periodicity apply?

components.interpretation: No operational reference-orientation detector is specified for the implementation being evaluated.
open_questions: What exact detector establishes canonical orientation?

components.interpretation: Translation is centered on the reference point and acquisition is assumed upright; image canonicalization is not specified, while matching uses approximate template rotations.
open_questions: Is any image resampling performed before the 11.25-degree enrollment variant?
What canvas, center, interpolation, and border rule are used?

components.interpretation: For the 640-component MSU setting: one excluded 20-pixel center circle, five retained 20-pixel bands, sixteen sectors per band, and outer radius 120 pixels at 500 dpi.
i=0..79 is band-major: Ti=i div 16 and theta_i=(i mod 16)*2pi/16; radial lower bounds are inclusive and upper bounds exclusive.
Each sector is normalized separately before filtering with desired mean M0=100 and desired variance V0=100.
Even-symmetric Gaussian-windowed cosine with the source coordinate rotation.
Eight directions relative to the x-axis: 0, 22.5, 45, 67.5, 90, 112.5, 135, and 157.5 degrees.
At 500 dpi f=1/K with average inter-ridge distance K approximately 10 pixels; the CVPR filter example freezes f=0.1 cycles/pixel.
Spatial-domain 33x33 mask; convolution uses only mask samples whose absolute value is greater than 0.05. Image/ROI boundary behavior remains unspecified under preprocessing.
V_i_theta=(1/n_i)*sum over sector pixels of abs(F_i_theta(x,y)-P_i_theta), where P is the sector mean of the filtered response.

80 sectors times 8 orientations gives 640 components and V_i_theta identifiers, but the linear serialization order and the mapping/clipping/rounding from AAD values to 256 one-byte values are not specified.
open_questions: Is orientation-major or sector-major serialization authoritative?
What exact quantizer produces the claimed 640-byte FingerCode?
Is Euclidean distance computed on raw or quantized values?

components.interpretation: The paper specifies five cyclic templates at R=-2..2 plus five from an 11.25-degree rotated enrollment image and takes the minimum of ten distances, but does not specify rotation resampling, canvas, border, or resulting preprocessing.
open_questions: What exact 11.25-degree image rotation operator is used?
How are rotated pixels outside the source handled?

components.interpretation: Corresponding FingerCodes are compared by Euclidean distance; the final distance is the minimum across ten enrollment templates, so lower is more similar. Exact numeric reproducibility still depends on the unresolved vector quantization.

decision.trigger: At least one key parameter is ambiguous or absent; the request explicitly forbids substituting a different core/reference/orientation/rotation method.

hash_scope: canonical compact sorted-key JSON of this object with specification_completeness_sha256 omitted
